content:
metadata:
  archetype: multi-surface Rust application with shared core runtime, app-server transport, and interactive TUI/CLI frontends
  version: 1
  drafted_by: synth_stages
stages:
- id: stage-1
  title: Process entrypoints and binary dispatch
  description: 'Native binaries start here: the top-level codex CLI, TUI launchers, app-server binaries, exec-mode binaries, helper tools, and small standalone utilities. This stage covers argv[0]-based dispatch, clap parsing, subcommand selection, helper-binary entrypoints, and the first handoff from process startup into a concrete runtime mode.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-1.1, stage-1.2]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-1.1
  title: Primary user-facing launch surfaces
  description: Covers the main executable surfaces that choose between interactive TUI, non-interactive exec, app-server tooling, remote-control commands, sandbox commands, desktop-app launch, doctor/debug flows, and related top-level command routing.
  parent: stage-1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-1.2
  title: Auxiliary binaries and developer tools
  description: Covers standalone binaries that are invoked directly rather than through the main CLI, such as apply_patch, file-search, mcp-server, responses-api-proxy, stdio bridges, schema generators, state log viewers, protocol exporters, and sample/test helper binaries.
  parent: stage-1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-2
  title: Early process hardening and runtime bootstrap
  description: Before the application does meaningful work, it applies process-hardening, installs global crypto/TLS providers, prepares PATH aliasing and runtime environment, initializes Tokio/runtime scaffolding, and performs other one-time bootstrap needed by later phases.
  parent: null
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-3
  title: Installation context, home discovery, and local environment probing
  description: The process determines where it is installed, where CODEX_HOME and bundled resources live, what terminal or OS environment it is running under, and which local helper binaries or platform capabilities are available. This stage establishes the filesystem and platform context used by config loading and startup decisions.
  parent: null
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-4
  title: Configuration, feature resolution, and startup policy assembly
  description: The system loads layered configuration and requirements from managed, cloud, user, project, thread, and CLI sources; resolves feature flags; compiles permission profiles and sandbox policy; imports built-in assets; and produces the effective runtime configuration that governs all later behavior.
  parent: null
  children: [stage-4.1, stage-4.2]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-4.1
  title: Config layer ingestion and requirements composition
  description: Reads TOML and managed sources, composes requirements layers, validates trust-gated project config, applies strict validation, and builds source-aware effective config and requirements stacks.
  parent: stage-4
  children: [stage-4.1.1, stage-4.1.2, stage-4.1.3]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-4.2
  title: Feature flags, provider catalogs, and built-in asset installation
  description: Resolves feature toggles, model-provider definitions, approval presets, collaboration templates, built-in skills, and other startup assets that must exist before sessions or UI surfaces can be created.
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-5
  title: Authentication, identity, and account readiness
  description: The runtime loads persisted auth state, performs login or token refresh when needed, resolves provider-specific auth, prepares agent identity or request-signing helpers, and establishes whether backend- or provider-backed features are available for the chosen mode.
  parent: null
  children: [stage-5.1, stage-5.2]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-5.1
  title: Interactive and persisted login flows
  description: Covers browser login, device-code login, auth storage backends, logout/revocation, keyring or encrypted-secret persistence, and onboarding-time auth UX.
  parent: stage-5
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-5.2
  title: Provider and backend auth adaptation
  description: Builds bearer-token, Bedrock, AWS SigV4, agent-identity, remote-control, and backend-client auth adapters so later HTTP, websocket, and RPC clients can authenticate requests consistently.
  parent: stage-5
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-6
  title: Persistence and local runtime services startup
  description: The application opens and migrates local SQLite state, rollout storage, thread stores, message history, graph stores, and related local persistence services. It also starts background maintenance such as rollout compression, state backfill, and log persistence so higher layers can rely on durable storage.
  parent: null
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-7
  title: Backend clients, remote catalogs, and startup refreshes
  description: With config and auth available, the process initializes outbound clients for Codex/ChatGPT backends, cloud config, model catalogs, plugin catalogs, connector directories, remote skills, and similar startup-time remote dependencies. This stage fetches or refreshes the external metadata needed before serving requests or rendering rich UI.
  parent: null
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-8
  title: Transport and server runtime initialization
  description: 'Server-capable modes bring up JSON-RPC transports and connection infrastructure: stdio, websocket, Unix-socket, remote-control, daemon-managed app-server processes, exec-server sessions, MCP server loops, and related connection registries. This stage establishes the channels over which requests and notifications will flow.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-8.1, stage-8.2]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-8.1
  title: App-server and daemon transport bring-up
  description: Initializes app-server transports, remote-control enrollment/reconnect machinery, daemon-managed process backends, websocket auth policy, stdio routing, and client/server facades used by CLI and TUI callers.
  parent: stage-8
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-8.2
  title: Execution and integration sidecar servers
  description: Starts exec-server handlers, noise relay plumbing, stdio-to-UDS bridges, responses proxy loops, MCP server dispatch, and other sidecar-style runtimes that expose Codex capabilities over dedicated protocols.
  parent: stage-8
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-9
  title: Frontend session startup and user-facing initialization
  description: 'Interactive frontends now attach to the initialized backend/runtime: the TUI starts its app state and terminal integration, onboarding may run, keymaps and notifications are prepared, and non-interactive exec mode constructs its session and output pipeline. This stage turns initialized services into a ready user-facing surface.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-9.1, stage-9.2]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-9.1
  title: TUI startup, onboarding, and terminal ownership
  description: Covers run_main/run_ratatui_app, App::run startup, onboarding flows, terminal capability setup, suspend/resume support, clipboard and notification backend selection, and initial thread/session attachment for the interactive UI.
  parent: stage-9
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-9.2
  title: Exec-mode and scripted session startup
  description: Covers codex exec startup, prompt sourcing, resume selection, JSONL output setup, and other non-interactive frontends that prepare one session and then drive it to completion.
  parent: stage-9
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-10
  title: Main event loop and request dispatch
  description: 'Once running, the system enters its steady-state dispatch loop: UI events are handled, JSON-RPC requests are decoded, notifications are routed, thread-scoped channels are selected, and request processors decide which subsystem should handle each incoming command or user action.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-10.1, stage-10.2]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-10.1
  title: Interactive event dispatch
  description: Covers App::handle_event, keyboard and paste handling, slash-command parsing, popup routing, thread switching, request-user-input overlays, mention search, and other TUI-side event dispatch paths.
  parent: stage-10
  children: [stage-10.1.1, stage-10.1.2, stage-10.1.3, stage-10.1.4]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-10.2
  title: RPC request routing
  description: Covers app-server message processing, request_processors, MCP dispatch, exec-server RPC handlers, and other typed request routers that translate protocol messages into concrete runtime operations.
  parent: stage-10
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-11
  title: Thread and session orchestration
  description: Dispatched work is bound to a conversation thread or session. This stage creates, resumes, reloads, archives, restores, or switches threads; reconstructs history from rollout/state; manages thread metadata and status; and assembles the long-lived session object that owns turn execution and event delivery.
  parent: null
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-12
  title: Prompt, context, and extension assembly
  description: 'Before a turn is sent to a model, the runtime assembles model-visible context: conversation history, environment fragments, permissions prompts, skills, memories, goals, apps/connectors, IDE context, user instructions, and extension-contributed prompt fragments. This stage produces the exact prompt/input state for the next unit of work.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-12.1, stage-12.2, stage-12.3, stage-12.4]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-13
  title: Turn execution and model interaction
  description: 'The core session turn driver runs the main unit of work: compaction decisions, hook processing, request construction, streaming model calls over HTTP/SSE/WebSocket/realtime transports, retry/fallback behavior, and turn completion bookkeeping. This is the central request/response loop of the system.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-13.1, stage-13.2]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-13.1
  title: Model transport execution
  description: Covers Responses API clients, realtime websocket/webrtc clients, provider-specific request shaping, streaming decoders, error mapping, and rollout tracing around outbound inference requests.
  parent: stage-13
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-13.2
  title: Streaming reduction and UI projection
  description: Covers incremental stream parsing, markdown/table holdback, transcript commit policy, chat widget live-cell updates, status-line changes, and conversion of low-level events into frontend-visible transcript state.
  parent: stage-13
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14
  title: Tool execution, approvals, and guarded side effects
  description: When model output requests actions, the runtime resolves tool specs, enforces approval and sandbox policy, runs hooks, executes local or remote tools, and converts results back into protocol items. This stage includes shell execution, apply_patch, MCP tools, code mode, web/image/memory/skills tools, and guarded approval workflows.
  parent: null
  children: [stage-14.1, stage-14.2, stage-14.3, stage-14.4]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.1
  title: Approval, guardian, and hook mediation
  description: Handles request_permissions flows, guardian review sessions, hook discovery/execution, stop/continue decisions, and policy checks that gate whether a requested side effect may proceed.
  parent: stage-14
  children: [stage-14.1.1, stage-14.1.2, stage-14.1.3, stage-14.1.4, stage-14.1.5, stage-14.1.6]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.2
  title: Execution backends and sandboxed command runtimes
  description: Runs shell/unified-exec/code-mode/apply_patch and related command-like tools through local PTY/pipe backends, exec-server remoting, Linux/macOS/Windows sandbox launchers, shell escalation, and process/session managers.
  parent: stage-14
  children: [stage-14.2.1, stage-14.2.2, stage-14.2.3, stage-14.2.4, stage-14.2.5, stage-14.2.6]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.3
  title: Extension and integration tools
  description: Executes extension-backed tools and integrations such as MCP resources, memories tools, skills tools, web search, image generation, plugin-install prompts, connector/app discovery, and other non-core tool namespaces.
  parent: stage-14
  children: [stage-14.3.1, stage-14.3.2, stage-14.3.3, stage-14.3.4]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-15
  title: Multi-agent, collaboration, and background workflows
  description: Some turns spawn or coordinate additional workers rather than finishing in one linear path. This stage covers subagent lifecycle management, mailbox-style agent messaging, review-mode agents, goal continuation, agent jobs over CSV, memory extraction pipelines, and other background or multi-threaded workflows layered on top of the core session loop.
  parent: null
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-16
  title: Result persistence, projection, and user-visible state updates
  description: After work completes or advances, the system persists rollout items, updates thread metadata and state databases, emits notifications, refreshes summaries/status projections, updates TUI transcript/history cells, and records machine-readable outputs for exec or external clients.
  parent: null
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-17
  title: Shutdown, cleanup, and teardown
  description: When a session, connection, or process ends, the runtime drains pending events, shuts down transports, terminates child processes, releases sandbox or sleep-inhibition resources, flushes persistence queues, closes sessions, and performs bounded graceful shutdown for frontends, servers, and helper daemons.
  parent: null
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-18
  title: Protocol schemas, shared types, and generated contracts
  description: 'Shared wire contracts span every lifecycle phase: Codex protocol types, app-server protocol schemas, JSON-RPC envelopes, code-mode protocol, backend OpenAPI models, thread-store models, plugin models, and generated bindings used by clients, servers, persistence, and tooling.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-18.1, stage-18.2, stage-18.3, stage-18.4, stage-18.5]
  crosscut: true
- id: stage-19
  title: Cross-cutting transport, networking, and client infrastructure
  description: 'Reusable transport and networking layers support many phases: HTTP/websocket clients, TLS and custom CA handling, cookie policy, UDS abstractions, network proxying, remote HTTP forwarding, backend clients, and generic request/response helpers shared across frontends and servers.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-19.1, stage-19.2, stage-19.3, stage-19.4]
  crosscut: true
- id: stage-20
  title: Cross-cutting observability, analytics, and feedback
  description: Logging, tracing, metrics, analytics reduction, rollout tracing, debug capture, feedback packaging, response-debug metadata, and state-backed log persistence operate across startup, request handling, streaming, tool execution, and shutdown.
  parent: null
  children: [stage-20.1, stage-20.2, stage-20.3, stage-20.4, stage-20.5]
  crosscut: true
- id: stage-21
  title: Cross-cutting persistence abstractions and data stores
  description: 'Beyond startup and writeback points, several storage abstractions are used throughout the system: rollout files, SQLite state runtime, thread stores, agent graph stores, message history, secrets/keyring storage, cloud-config caches, remote plugin caches, and memory stores.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-21.1, stage-21.2, stage-21.3, stage-21.4, stage-21.5]
  crosscut: true
- id: stage-22
  title: Cross-cutting utility and support libraries
  description: 'General-purpose helpers used throughout the codebase live here: path and URI types, string and truncation helpers, image utilities, fuzzy matching, async helpers, readiness primitives, template rendering, ANSI-to-TUI adapters, filesystem abstractions, sandbox summaries, terminal detection, and similar reusable support crates.'
  parent: null
  children: [stage-22.1, stage-22.2, stage-22.3, stage-22.4, stage-22.5, stage-22.6]
  crosscut: true
- id: stage-23
  title: Testing, fixtures, and developer verification harnesses
  description: Repository-wide test harnesses, integration fixtures, fake servers, smoke-test clients, snapshot suites, and helper binaries validate every lifecycle phase. This stage covers the dedicated tests/ trees and support crates that exercise startup, request handling, streaming, persistence, and teardown behavior.
  parent: null
  children: [stage-23.1, stage-23.2, stage-23.3, stage-23.4, stage-23.5, stage-23.6]
  crosscut: true
- id: stage-18.1
  title: Core shared protocol and domain types
  description: Handwritten shared protocol/domain models, identifiers, config-visible enums, plugin/tool/thread-store schemas, and closely related cross-crate type definitions centered on the main protocol crate and other broadly reused contract crates.
  parent: stage-18
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-18.2
  title: App-server protocol schemas and transport contracts
  description: 'The app-server protocol family: JSON-RPC envelopes, schema export tooling, shared/common protocol glue, versioned v1/v2 request and notification types, and tightly coupled app-server transport/error contract files.'
  parent: stage-18
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-18.3
  title: Generated backend and protobuf contracts
  description: Generated or schema-derived bindings, especially backend OpenAPI model types and protobuf/tonic relay or thread-config bindings, grouped apart from handwritten protocol code.
  parent: stage-18
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-18.4
  title: API, extension, hook, MCP, and trace schemas
  description: 'Other contract-heavy schema surfaces outside the main protocol crates: public API payloads, code-mode protocol types, extension and hook interfaces, MCP-facing shared types, exec event wire schemas, and rollout-trace models.'
  parent: stage-18
  children: [stage-18.4.1, stage-18.4.2, stage-18.4.3, stage-18.4.4, stage-18.4.5, stage-18.4.6]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-22.1
  title: Path, filesystem, environment, and sandbox support utilities
  description: Reusable helpers for path and URI handling, filesystem abstractions, environment shaping, executable resolution, terminal/process environment detection, and sandbox/platform-specific support code.
  parent: stage-22
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-22.2
  title: Text, parsing, truncation, and rendering helpers
  description: General-purpose text-processing, parsing, wrapping, truncation, formatting, templating, stream parsing, and lightweight render/layout helpers shared across the codebase and especially TUI/CLI presentation code.
  parent: stage-22
  children: [stage-22.2.1, stage-22.2.2, stage-22.2.3, stage-22.2.4, stage-22.2.5]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-22.3
  title: Configuration, metadata, schema, auth, and network glue utilities
  description: Support helpers for configuration normalization and diagnostics, metadata shaping, schema generation, auth flows, host/policy normalization, and small API-facing adapters that are reused outside a single feature implementation.
  parent: stage-22
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-22.4
  title: Shell, command, git, plugin, and execution support utilities
  description: Reusable support code for shell parsing and safety checks, git and plugin packaging helpers, command/tool execution support, command-output buffering, and related runtime-facing utilities used by multiple higher-level subsystems.
  parent: stage-22
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-22.5
  title: Async primitives, image handling, and miscellaneous small support libraries
  description: Small standalone utility crates and helpers for async cancellation/readiness, image ingestion and encoding, cacheing, memory parsing, sleep inhibition, runtime value conversion, and compact human-readable sandbox summaries.
  parent: stage-22
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.1
  title: App-server test suites and protocol verification
  description: 'App-server-focused test code: protocol serde/schema regression tests, app-server unit and integration suites, app-server transport checks, daemon updater/backend tests, the dedicated app-server test client, and shared app-server test fixtures.'
  parent: stage-23
  children: [stage-23.1.1, stage-23.1.2, stage-23.1.3, stage-23.1.4]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2
  title: Core runtime and session test harnesses
  description: Core crate unit, integration, and end-to-end verification for sessions, tools, prompts, transport, approvals, compaction, realtime, plugins, rollout/state handling, plus the shared core integration harness under core/tests.
  parent: stage-23
  children: [stage-23.2.1, stage-23.2.2, stage-23.2.3, stage-23.2.4]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.3
  title: CLI, exec, login, and MCP server developer verification
  description: Executable-facing verification for CLI commands, codex-exec flows, login/logout journeys, apply-patch integration tests, legacy execpolicy suites, and MCP server integration harnesses used to validate developer workflows.
  parent: stage-23
  children: [stage-23.3.1, stage-23.3.2, stage-23.3.3, stage-23.3.4, stage-23.3.5, stage-23.3.6]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.4
  title: Exec-server, sandbox, and remote transport harnesses
  description: Test harnesses for exec-server RPCs, relay and Noise transport, filesystem and process backends, sandbox runners, Windows/Linux execution bridges, remote-control transport, and related path/UDS plumbing.
  parent: stage-23
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.5
  title: TUI interaction and rendering tests
  description: 'Terminal UI and chat-widget verification: TUI app orchestration tests, VT100 and rendering snapshots, command and popup behavior, status surfaces, layout logic, and integration smoke tests under the tui test trees.'
  parent: stage-23
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.6
  title: Cross-cutting library tests, fixtures, and telemetry or rollout support
  description: Crate-local verification for shared libraries and subsystems outside the main app-server/core/TUI/exec-server buckets, including analytics, config, plugins, memories, telemetry, rollout/state, utilities, extension crates, and support fixtures used by those tests.
  parent: stage-23
  children: [stage-23.6.1, stage-23.6.2, stage-23.6.3, stage-23.6.4, stage-23.6.5, stage-23.6.6]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-12.1
  title: Prompt and context facade modules, fragments, and embedded instruction templates
  description: Covers unassigned files that define prompt-fragment crate roots, embedded collaboration/home instruction sources, and core context facade modules used when assembling model-visible context.
  parent: stage-12
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.4
  title: Sandbox policy generation and command-safety parsing helpers
  description: 'Collects unassigned files directly supporting tool-execution confinement and approval decisions: sandbox build/policy helpers plus shell-command parsing and safety classifiers.'
  parent: stage-14
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.1.1
  title: Protocol schema and wire-format verification
  description: Protocol-layer regression tests and schema fixture generation checks for app-server JSON-RPC and remote-control wire formats.
  parent: stage-23.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.1.2
  title: Daemon, transport, and test-client support tests
  description: Tests and support code for the app-server daemon backend/updater behavior, transport-layer checks, and the dedicated app-server test client plus plugin-analytics helpers.
  parent: stage-23.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.1.3
  title: App-server unit tests and shared integration fixtures
  description: App-server crate unit tests plus reusable integration-test fixtures, harnesses, and suite indexes that support the larger end-to-end test tree.
  parent: stage-23.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.1.4
  title: App-server integration suites
  description: End-to-end app-server integration tests covering auth, config, search, summaries, and the full v2 JSON-RPC/API surface including threads, turns, plugins, MCP, transport, remote control, and related behaviors.
  parent: stage-23.1
  children: [stage-23.1.4.1, stage-23.1.4.2, stage-23.1.4.3, stage-23.1.4.4]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.1
  title: Core src runtime, session, policy, and state tests
  description: Reassigns the stage-23.2 core/src verification files that exercise agent/session lifecycle, prompt/context shaping, approvals and execution policy, compaction, guardian, realtime, plugins, rollout/state handling, transport helpers, and other non-tools runtime behavior. This substage is an internal source-level test group under the core crate.
  parent: stage-23.2
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.2
  title: Core src tools and unified-exec tests
  description: Reassigns the stage-23.2 core/src/tools and core/src/unified_exec verification files that cover tool specs, handlers, router and registry behavior, approvals and sandboxing helpers, tool dispatch tracing, runtime preparation, and unified-exec internals.
  parent: stage-23.2
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.3
  title: Core integration harness and common test support
  description: Reassigns the shared core/tests harness, helpers, and integration-test infrastructure files that underpin end-to-end verification for the core crate. This substage groups reusable support code rather than feature suites themselves.
  parent: stage-23.2
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.4
  title: Core end-to-end session, transport, tool, and feature suites
  description: Reassigns the feature-oriented core/tests/suite end-to-end test files that validate live session behavior, transport and provider interactions, approvals, compaction, hooks, realtime, remote environments, tools, plugins, rollout/state persistence, and related user-visible runtime flows.
  parent: stage-23.2
  children: [stage-23.2.4.1, stage-23.2.4.2, stage-23.2.4.3, stage-23.2.4.4, stage-23.2.4.5, stage-23.2.4.6]
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.6.1
  title: Analytics and telemetry tests
  description: Tests and support fixtures for analytics event generation, telemetry helpers, OpenTelemetry metrics/logging/export, and tracing-related observability behavior.
  parent: stage-23.6
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.6.2
  title: Configuration, policy, and environment tests
  description: Crate-local tests for config parsing/loading/merging, cloud-config behavior, permissions and sandbox policy semantics, feature resolution, hook and prompt policy rendering, and related environment/config fixtures.
  parent: stage-23.6
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.6.3
  title: Plugins, extensions, skills, MCP, and tools tests
  description: Tests for plugin discovery/loading/marketplace flows, extension APIs and extension crates, MCP integration and transports, skills management, and tool-definition or tool-conversion behavior.
  parent: stage-23.6
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.6.4
  title: API clients, models, protocol, prompts, and transport support tests
  description: Tests and close support fixtures for API/backend clients, model/provider registries and caches, protocol encoding/decoding, prompt rendering, transport helpers, sockets, and client-side mock/support crates used by those tests.
  parent: stage-23.6
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.6.5
  title: Memories, rollout, state, and persistence tests
  description: Tests and support fixtures for memories pipelines, rollout recording and trace reduction, runtime/state persistence, message and thread storage, external-agent session ledgers, and filesystem-backed recovery behavior.
  parent: stage-23.6
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-18.5
  title: API annotation macros and compile-time contract support
  description: Compile-time code that defines or enforces shared API/contract semantics rather than runtime behavior, including procedural macros that generate experimental-API checks and registrations.
  parent: stage-18
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-22.6
  title: Build scripts and build-time asset/platform glue
  description: Crosscutting Cargo build scripts and other compile-time support files that manage platform linker flags, embedded asset change detection, vendored native compilation, and manifest embedding.
  parent: stage-22
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.2.1
  title: Execution-facing app-server and core command orchestration
  description: Execution-specific bridges from app-server requests and core tool/task entry points into command execution, apply_patch dispatch, shell execution, and unified-exec runtime launch paths. This excludes generic hook infrastructure and low-level process/session backends.
  parent: stage-14.2
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.2.2
  title: Unified-exec sessions and PTY/process backends
  description: Low-level interactive process machinery for unified-exec sessions, including process wrappers and managers, exec-server process abstractions, and PTY/pipe implementations across Unix and Windows.
  parent: stage-14.2
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.2.3
  title: Patch application engine and patch-execution adapters
  description: Patch parsing and application logic plus the core/tool/runtime adapters that vet and execute apply_patch operations as a command-like capability.
  parent: stage-14.2
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.2.4
  title: Sandbox selection and Unix platform launchers
  description: Shared sandbox-selection and request-rewriting logic plus Unix-oriented launcher integrations for Linux and macOS sandboxed execution, including debug surfaces and shell escalation protocol support.
  parent: stage-14.2
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.2.5
  title: Exec-server filesystem sandbox services
  description: Exec-server support for file reads and filesystem operations, including local and sandboxed filesystem backends and helper-mediated filesystem sandbox execution.
  parent: stage-14.2
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.2.6
  title: Windows sandbox provisioning and process-launch internals
  description: Windows-specific sandbox identity, setup, ACL/token management, elevated runner plumbing, process creation, stdio bridging, and related internals for sandboxed command execution.
  parent: stage-14.2
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.3.1
  title: MCP runtime, resources, and session integration
  description: 'Model Context Protocol integration surfaces: CLI/config management, live connection management, auth elicitation, MCP tool and resource execution, session refresh/orchestration, and MCP-backed extension registration.'
  parent: stage-14.3
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.3.2
  title: Plugin and connector ecosystem management
  description: Plugin marketplaces, loading, installation/removal, remote bundles and sharing, connector/app discovery and policy, install-suggestion tooling, and related CLI/TUI integration surfaces.
  parent: stage-14.3
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.3.3
  title: Extension-backed tool runtimes and namespaces
  description: Concrete extension-backed and non-core tool namespaces plus their adapters, including dynamic tools, extension tool execution, code-mode integration, memories, skills, web search, image generation, goal tools, and related search/view helpers.
  parent: stage-14.3
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.3.4
  title: App-server integration discovery and search adapters
  description: App-server adapters that expose integration-backed discovery and search capabilities to clients, including apps listing over connectors/MCP/plugin catalogs and interactive fuzzy file search session handling.
  parent: stage-14.3
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.4.1
  title: Transport, streaming, and provider protocol suites
  description: End-to-end tests for Codex transport behavior and provider-facing protocol details, including HTTP/SSE/WebSocket flows, headers, compression, retries, proxying, turn-state propagation, transport fallback, and other request/response mechanics at the API boundary.
  parent: stage-23.2.4
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.4.2
  title: Session history, compaction, resume, and persisted state suites
  description: Tests for conversation continuity and stored runtime state across turns and restarts, including compaction, pending input, resume and fork behavior, rollout persistence, thread-setting updates, review-thread history, and SQLite or rollout-backed session data.
  parent: stage-23.2.4
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.4.3
  title: Model request shaping, prompt assembly, and runtime model-selection suites
  description: 'Suites focused on what Codex sends to the model or derives from model metadata: prompt/input assembly, contextual instruction injection, model-visible layout, provider request shaping, model switching, personality and permissions messaging, prompt caching, remote model metadata, and runtime selector behavior.'
  parent: stage-23.2.4
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.4.4
  title: Multi-agent, collaboration, and remote-environment suites
  description: End-to-end tests for distributed and collaborative runtime behavior, including spawned agents, hierarchical instructions, delegated events, agent-job execution, subagent notifications, remote environments, and mailbox or thread coordination across parent and child sessions.
  parent: stage-23.2.4
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.4.5
  title: Approvals, permissions, hooks, and review-mediation suites
  description: 'Tests centered on runtime mediation and policy gates around user-visible actions: approvals, permission requests and grants, auto-review and Guardian review routing, request-user-input flows, hook interception, notifications, and safety or reviewer metadata attached to tool and MCP execution.'
  parent: stage-23.2.4
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.2.4.6
  title: Tool, shell/exec, MCP/app, plugin, and runtime item suites
  description: End-to-end suites for tool exposure and execution behavior, including shell and exec tools, apply-patch and custom tools, MCP/app and plugin integrations, search and image tooling, truncation and serialization of tool outputs, tool parallelism, user-invoked shell commands, and emitted runtime items/events.
  parent: stage-23.2.4
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.6.6
  title: Utility crate tests for path/URI and output truncation helpers
  description: Crate-local verification for shared utility libraries that are not tied to a single runtime flow, especially path/URI semantics and output-truncation behavior.
  parent: stage-23.6
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-10.1.1
  title: App-level event dispatch and thread routing
  description: Core TUI app orchestration for dispatching app events, handling global input, routing app-server events and requests, and switching or replaying thread-scoped interactive state.
  parent: stage-10.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-10.1.2
  title: Bottom-pane composer, popups, and mention input
  description: Bottom-pane input machinery centered on the chat composer, slash-command parsing, mention/file-search popups, reusable selection views, and modal bottom-pane request/setting flows.
  parent: stage-10.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-10.1.3
  title: Chat widget interaction and command flows
  description: ChatWidget-side interaction handling, slash-command dispatch, protocol-driven UI updates, queued input restoration, popup construction, usage/review/settings flows, and main chat rendering behavior.
  parent: stage-10.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-10.1.4
  title: Specialized interactive flows and auxiliary TUI handlers
  description: Focused interactive subsystems adjacent to event dispatch, including cloud-tasks UI state, backtracking, keymap setup, pets, multi-agent navigation, clipboard/external import helpers, and related picker flows.
  parent: stage-10.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-12.2
  title: Context fragment definitions and prompt assets
  description: Low-level model-visible context fragment types, contextual message adapters, and embedded prompt/template assets that define the raw prompt content shapes used during assembly.
  parent: stage-12
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-12.3
  title: Instruction, skill, plugin, memory, and review prompt contributors
  description: Prompt-contribution producers that load, select, render, or inject model-visible instructions from user config, project files, skills, plugins, memories, goals, and review flows.
  parent: stage-12
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-12.4
  title: Turn context, history, and realtime prompt assembly
  description: Assembly of the exact next-turn model input from session state, turn-context snapshots, transcript history, incremental updates, realtime startup context, and debug prompt construction.
  parent: stage-12
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-20.1
  title: Analytics event modeling, reduction, and emitters
  description: 'The analytics subsystem itself: accepted-line parsing, fact and event schemas, reducer/client delivery, and small adapters that emit analytics payloads for app, goal, and auth-related contexts.'
  parent: stage-20
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-20.2
  title: OpenTelemetry runtime, provider, and metrics foundations
  description: Shared observability infrastructure for configuring, initializing, and exposing OpenTelemetry tracing/metrics/logging, including provider setup, trace context propagation, metrics clients, validation, naming, and transport telemetry callback traits.
  parent: stage-20
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-20.3
  title: Session telemetry and feature-specific instrumentation
  description: Session-scoped telemetry helpers plus instrumentation attached to specific product areas such as app-server tracing, cloud-config, guardian, goals, memories, sandbox tagging, tool dispatch tracing, startup/turn timing, and lightweight SQLite startup telemetry.
  parent: stage-20
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-20.4
  title: Rollout trace recording, schema, and replay reducers
  description: The rollout-trace crate for capturing raw execution traces, defining bundle and event schemas, writing payloads/events, and reducing traces into replayable conversation, thread, tool, and runtime models.
  parent: stage-20
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-20.5
  title: Feedback capture, debug artifacts, and log persistence
  description: Feedback assembly and diagnostics, safe response/debug metadata extraction, request/response dump capture, secret redaction, and local log capture/persistence layers that store or package debug information for later inspection.
  parent: stage-20
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.3.1
  title: apply-patch executable integration tests
  description: Executable-facing integration and scenario tests for the standalone apply-patch binary, focused on CLI invocation modes, filesystem outcomes, overwrite and rename behavior, and fixture-driven end-to-end patch application.
  parent: stage-23.3
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.3.2

  description: Command-entrypoint integration tests for the main codex CLI, covering app and exec server entrypoints, debug and maintenance commands, feature/config handling, plugin and marketplace subcommands, MCP management commands, execpolicy command output, login invocation, and update behavior.
  parent: stage-23.3
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.3.3

  description: Tests centered on the codex-exec executable surface, including CLI parsing, subprocess behavior, event/output formats, session persistence and resume, prompt sourcing, auth/header wiring, approval and MCP startup failures, hooks, and apply-patch-capable exec flows.

  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-23.3.4
  title: legacy and current execpolicy executable tests

  children: []
  crosscut: false

  crosscut: false

- id: stage-23.1.4.1

  children: []
  crosscut: false

  description: Integration tests for websocket/realtime transport, connection management, handshake metadata, client-visible protocol contracts, and notification or request mediation behavior that spans multiple app-server features.

  children: []
  crosscut: false

  children: []
  crosscut: false

  description: 'Integration tests for conversational state and execution lifecycle: thread creation, reading, listing, persistence, archive/delete/fork/resume flows, turn start/interrupt/steer behavior, review and compaction, thread stores and summaries, per-thread settings and metadata, and policy/status notifications tied to active sessions.'
  parent: stage-23.1.4
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-4.1.1

  description: Shared configuration-crate internals that define config TOML schemas, normalize and merge layered values, track provenance, validate strictly, ingest cloud/managed/user/project/thread sources, and assemble the effective configuration stack.
  parent: stage-4.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-4.1.2
  title: Requirements layering and execution-policy composition
  description: 'Files centered on requirements.toml ingestion and composition: requirement schemas, source-aware normalization, special per-field merge rules, full stack assembly, and parsing or amending execution-policy inputs consumed by requirements.'
  parent: stage-4.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-4.1.3
  title: Configuration management services and editable persistence surfaces
  description: 'Files that expose configuration ingestion results through management APIs or persist user-editable config state: app-server config read/write services and processors, config editing helpers, daemon-local settings persistence, and external-agent config import into Codex-managed locations.'
  parent: stage-4.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.1.1
  title: Approval policy and request-decision engines
  description: Core decision logic that determines whether commands, patches, network access, or sandbox permission changes may proceed, including current and legacy execution-policy evaluators plus approval-specific normalization and rule translation.
  parent: stage-14.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.1.2
  title: Guardian review and mediated approval sessions
  description: Guardian-specific approval request modeling, reviewer prompt construction, nested review execution, reusable review sessions, review-turn setup, and review-exit presentation for mediated approval decisions.
  parent: stage-14.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.1.3
  title: Hook execution and stop-continue mediation
  description: Hook runtime infrastructure and event-specific handlers that discover, run, and aggregate lifecycle/tool/permission hooks into stop, block, continue, or context-injection outcomes.
  parent: stage-14.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.1.4
  title: Permission and elicitation request ingress
  description: Front-door request flows that accept permission, elicitation, and structured user-input requests from tools or MCP integrations, normalize them, and hand them into the approval and review machinery.
  parent: stage-14.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.1.5
  title: Approval-mediated tool orchestration and approval UI
  description: Runtime orchestration and user-facing TUI surfaces for approval-driven tool execution, approval dialogs, permission selection, hook inspection, pending-request surfacing, and structured user-response collection.
  parent: stage-14.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-14.1.6
  title: Approval-adjacent enforcement runtimes
  description: Subsystem runtime and OS-enforcement components that apply or host policy decisions at execution time, including live proxy policy state and Windows sandbox/WFP protection setup used by approval-gated flows.
  parent: stage-14.1
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-19.1
  title: Generic HTTP client, TLS, cookies, and streaming transport foundations
  description: Reusable outbound networking foundations centered on reqwest-based transport, retry/backoff, custom CA handling, constrained cookie storage, request/response wrappers, SSE decoding, and closely related authenticated HTTP helpers and library facades for backend-facing clients.
  parent: stage-19
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-19.2
  title: App-server, exec-server, and relay transport channels
  description: Shared transport channels used to move messages, HTTP payloads, files, and encrypted relay frames between processes and services, covering app-server transport primitives, remote-control chunking and client management, exec-server HTTP/body-stream plumbing, remote file transport, and Noise/websocket relay layers.
  parent: stage-19
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-19.3
  title: Managed proxying and local IPC transport substrates
  description: Transport substrates for proxying and host-local communication, including managed network-proxy enforcement and upstream transport, transport-specific proxy configuration contracts, sandbox proxy routing, Unix-domain and named-pipe abstractions, shell-escalation socket transport, and IDE or elevated-runner IPC channels.
  parent: stage-19
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-19.4
  title: MCP and executor-backed transport adapters
  description: Adapters that bridge higher-level MCP client flows onto concrete transport mechanisms, including executor-managed process stdio, in-process transport creation, HTTP adaptation onto the shared executor HTTP capability, auth-header parsing for transport failures, retry around streamable HTTP initialization, and the RMCP client library facade.
  parent: stage-19
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-21.1
  title: Rollout files and thread-store persistence
  description: Filesystem-backed rollout/session persistence plus the thread-store abstraction and local implementation that record, read, list, search, and delete persisted conversation threads, including rollout/state parity diagnostics and global message-history storage.
  parent: stage-21
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-21.2
  title: SQLite runtime state and agent graph storage
  description: SQLite-backed runtime persistence for thread metadata, goals, jobs, memories, logs, remote-control records, and import tracking, together with the agent-graph store abstraction that persists spawn-edge relationships via the shared state runtime.
  parent: stage-21
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-21.3
  title: Caches and local persisted lookup data
  description: On-disk caches and small persisted lookup files used across subsystems for faster refreshes or local fallback behavior, including cloud-config, connector, model, plugin-catalog, shared-plugin-path, and update caches.
  parent: stage-21
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-21.4
  title: Plugin, secrets, and memory file stores
  description: Concrete local file-backed stores that own durable data layouts for plugins, encrypted secrets, and memories, along with the write-side memory workspace materialization and cleanup logic.
  parent: stage-21
  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-21.5
  title: External session import persistence
  description: Persistence and parsing support for importing external agent session files, including the ledger that remembers previously imported file versions and the record readers that normalize source session content for Codex.
  parent: stage-21
  children: []
  crosscut: false

  description: Public schema and trait surfaces for the code-mode protocol crate, covering tool descriptions, runtime request/response payloads, session contracts, response content types, and crate-level exports.

- id: stage-22.2.1

  description: 'Cross-crate text and value presentation helpers that are not TUI-specific: number and duration formatting, string/JSON utilities, truncation helpers, strict templating, CLI display strings, response-history trimming, and narrow action-detail formatting.'

  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-22.2.2

  description: Incremental parsing utilities for streamed text or bytes, including UTF-8 decoding, line framing, hidden-tag extraction, tagged block parsing, and citation extraction built on those generic parser primitives.

  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-22.2.3

  description: 'Low-level terminal-text helpers used by TUI rendering: ANSI-to-text conversion, width measurement, wrapping, truncation, hyperlink-preserving layout, markdown/table text shaping, scrollable wrapped diff views, and shared line/render composition primitives.'

  children: []
  crosscut: false
- id: stage-22.2.4

  description: 'Shared TUI-facing presentation helpers above the core text-layout layer: footer and popup text builders, selection and scroll state, key-hint and label formatting, style/color utilities, compact display-model shaping, and layout constants.'

  crosscut: false
